title: Quiz: Training Quantum GANs
description: Test your understanding of QGAN training techniques and optimization strategies

questions.question: What is the standard GAN generator loss?

options.text: -log(D(G(z))) to maximize discriminator output on fakes
Mean squared error between real and fake
Cross-entropy between real and fake labels

questions.explanation: The generator tries to maximize D(G(z)), equivalently minimizing -log(D(G(z))), so that the discriminator classifies fake samples as real.

questions.question: What is the advantage of Wasserstein loss over standard GAN loss?

options.text: It provides more stable gradients even when discriminator is strong
It eliminates the need for a discriminator
It only works with quantum circuits

questions.explanation: Wasserstein loss provides meaningful gradients even when the discriminator is well-trained, avoiding vanishing gradient problems that occur with standard GAN loss.

questions.question: Why is gradient clipping important in QGAN training?

options.text: To reduce the number of parameters
To prevent gradient explosion and stabilize training

questions.explanation: Gradient clipping bounds the gradient norm, preventing large updates that can destabilize training and cause the adversarial dynamics to diverge.

questions.question: What is a barren plateau in quantum circuit training?

options.text: A region where the circuit has too many qubits
A region where gradients vanish exponentially with circuit size
A hardware limitation of quantum devices

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus are regions in parameter space where the loss landscape becomes flat, with gradients that vanish exponentially with the number of qubits, making training extremely difficult.

questions.question: Label smoothing replaces hard labels (0,1) with soft labels (e.g., 0.1, 0.9) to prevent overconfident discriminator predictions.
questions.explanation: True. Label smoothing prevents the discriminator from becoming too confident, which can cause training instability and gradient issues for the generator.

questions.question: What indicates mode collapse in a QGAN?

options.text: The generator produces samples with very low variance

questions.explanation: Mode collapse occurs when the generator produces limited diversity, collapsing to a few modes. This is detected by low variance in generated samples.

questions.question: What is the ideal convergence state for D(real) and D(fake) in a well-trained GAN?
questions.explanation: At Nash equilibrium, the generator produces samples indistinguishable from real data, so the optimal discriminator outputs 0.5 for both, unable to differentiate them.

questions.question: Which techniques help mitigate barren plateaus? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Layerwise training (training one layer at a time)
Using local cost functions instead of global

questions.explanation: Layerwise training, identity initialization, and local cost functions all help avoid barren plateaus. Increasing circuit depth typically makes barren plateaus worse, not better.
